load_bearing_defect: D1 (Kotak cheque-deposit remark width ^[0-9]{3,5}$ -> {3,4}): a rare-trigger domain rule that fires only when the remark field is a bare 3-5 digit instrument (cheque) number; ordinary alphanumeric/empty remarks never feed it. Its correct upper bound 5 is pinned only by counting the three identical sibling occurrences of the same idiom. This is the analogue of the sibling's 16-char zero-prefixed cheque-remark rule that failed Opus 9/10.
litmus_result: PASS: leak check (git log --oneline | wc -l == 1, git status clean, git diff empty, all 5 planted markers present in built image). NULL run (--network none) reward 0 with all 5 f2p reporting per-test FAILED (no collection errors) and 14/14 p2p passing. ORACLE run (solve.sh) reward 1 with 19/19 required passing. PARTIAL run (revert only D1+D4) reward 0 with the other 3 f2p still FAILED. FAIRNESS run (materially different correct fixes: isin([1]) sentinel, != CURRENT account gate, sep_by_dash(4,6) final-segment slice, index=0 restoration) reward 1 / 19 pass.
base_defects_visible_suite: GREEN (the repo ships no test files; the agent-visible tree has nothing to run and no failing local test points at any defect). The gold boundary suite enters only at grade time via config.json test_patch; at base it is 19/19 green, at planted exactly the 5 f2p fail and all 14 p2p stay green.
env_note: No task-specific env, secrets, or network required. The per-bank categorization is deterministic and offline. Repo image (txenrich-repo:v1) is python:3.11-slim with numpy==1.26.4 / pandas==2.2.3 (plus scipy/scikit-learn/matplotlib/fuzzywuzzy/Levenshtein/flask) installed as wheels; the package is installed editable with --no-deps so the 2022-era setup.py pins (which have no cp311/arm64 wheels and fail to compile against modern numpy headers) are bypassed. The np.select string/amount logic runs unchanged on the modern pins; verified by exercising both bank categorizers directly.

defects.edge: a CREDIT whose remark is a bare 5-digit instrument (cheque) number (exactly the upper edge the accepted 3-5 digit width covers)
defects.derivability: The accepted width is pinned by three identical sibling occurrences of ^[0-9]{3,5}$ in the same file (the CHEQUE_PAID/DEBIT rule and two later CHEQUE_PAID cleaning rules), all keyed to the same 3-5 digit instrument-number width; the singleton {3,4} contradicts them and a grep of the remark idiom reveals the anomaly. Nothing in any test signposts it.
defects.wrong_output: the bare 5-digit-remark credit is no longer labelled CHEQUE_DEPOSIT (falls through to the default TRANSFER)

defects.edge: a rupee-one account-verification CREDIT (^Received from <NAME>, amount exactly 1)
defects.derivability: A sentinel-amount slip pinned by the pervasive amount.eq(1) idiom used 11 times across the repo to mark the rupee-one verification / penny-drop credits, and by the adjacent Karz-Xx4002 verification sibling on the line directly above (also amount.eq(1)). eq(2) matches no verification transaction; a grep of the sentinel idiom reveals the singleton.
defects.wrong_output: the rupee-one Received-from credit is no longer labelled ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION (falls through to IMPS)

defects.edge: an ACH salary CREDIT whose payee name is the final dash-segment of a NACH-SAL-CR-SAL-<NAME> layout
defects.derivability: A fixed-layout slice index pinned by the guard on the SAME line: the literal ^NACH-SAL-CR-SAL- has exactly four dash-delimited prefix tokens (NACH, SAL, CR, SAL) before the payee, so the payee is segment index 4 (the last). The adjacent sibling ^NACH-[A-Z0-9]+-CR- -> sep_by_dash(...,3,4) encodes the same layout->index mapping (three prefix tokens -> index 3). Shifting to segment 3 grabs the literal 'SAL' instead of the name.
defects.wrong_output: the payee name comes out as 'Sal' (a fixed layout token) instead of the actual employer name

defects.edge: a BULK POSTING-<NAME> credit (no digit / SALARY token) in a SAVING account
defects.derivability: A casing/spelling typo pinned by the repo-wide accountType == "SAVING" idiom (6 occurrences) and the adjacent "CURRENT" convention (4 occurrences). The caller never passes "SAVINGS", so the branch never fires; a grep of the account-type literal reveals the singleton.
defects.wrong_output: the savings-account bulk-posting payee is no longer extracted (partyName stays empty)

defects.edge: a TO TRANSFER INB <NAME> internet-banking DEBIT whose payee is the single capture group of its layout
defects.change: py_extract(transactions.description, pat="TO TRANSFER INB (.*)", index=0) -> index=1
defects.derivability: A capture-group index slip pinned by the same-line pattern: TO TRANSFER INB (.*) has exactly one capture group, so the only valid extract index is 0; index=1 references a non-existent group and py_extract returns empty. Pinned further by the pervasive index=0 idiom on single-group patterns across both scripts. Requires understanding py_extract's .get(index) semantics and counting the pattern's groups.
defects.wrong_output: the internet-banking payee is no longer extracted (partyName stays empty)

resistance_assessment: Five single-token slips across two of ~40 bank scripts (Kotak, SBI), each biting only an exact-width / sentinel-amount / fixed-layout / account-type edge that ordinary transactions never feed, so there is no failing local test and no run-pytest-see-red gradient (the repo ships no tests at all). A solve requires finding ALL five, which are deliberately five distinct shapes (a regex quantifier upper bound, an integer sentinel literal, a dash-slice segment index, a string-casing typo, a capture-group index) so no single pattern-match surfaces the rest. Two are easy-derivable (the amount.eq(1) sentinel greppable against 11 correct siblings and the SAVING casing against 6, p~0.9); three are medium (the ^[0-9]{3,5}$ width pinned only by counting/greping the three sibling occurrences, the NACH slice pinned only by the same-line dash-count layout plus the adjacent sibling, the INB capture-group index pinned only by the single-group pattern plus the index=0 idiom). D1 is the load-bearing rare-trigger: it fires only when the remark is a bare 3-5 digit instrument number, exactly the untested edge, the direct analogue of the sibling's 16-char cheque-remark rule that failed Opus 9/10. Targeting Opus ~1-4/10.
fairness_audit: Every gold test is pure-input: it builds a real pandas DataFrame of raw transaction fields and calls the actual categorize_Kotak_transactions / categorize_SBI_transactions, asserting on the returned category / subcategory / payee columns. There are ZERO mocks, ZERO patched attributes, and no reliance on any private helper name; a materially different but correct fix still passes (verified end-to-end: amount.isin([1]) for the sentinel, accountType != "CURRENT" for the account gate, sep_by_dash(4,6) for the final-segment slice, and index-0 restoration all reach reward 1). Each f2p is disjoint (different bank, branch and input) and each has a p2p that pins the adjacent correct behavior at both base and planted state (the 4-digit and 6-digit remarks for D1; the +500 non-sentinel and the Karz verification sibling for D2; the sibling NACH slice and the AUTO_CREDIT category for D3; the CURRENT-account and digit-token exclusions for D4; the INTERNET_BANKING category and the untouched UPI slice for D5). All five defected branches are reachable from the live categorizers (not dead paths). Deterministic and fully offline.
